components.content:
Welcome to your first Korean lesson! 

For the entirety of this course we will show Korean words in the Korean alphabet 한글 (Hangul) so this lesson is designed as a first introduction.
한글 has over 10,000 characters! Although, with only 2 lessons, you will be able to read, and write all these characters yourself.
But how is that possible? Is each lesson going to force you to memorize 5,000 characters each? Nope! You actually only need to know 24 characters. That's right, less than the English alphabet!
In this lesson, you will learn how to say 'I'm worried that I will ____' or 'I'm worried ____ will happen'
The standard grammatical particle for this is:
비가 올까 봐 우산을 가져올 거예요
This grammatical structure can be used with either 걱정하다 or 걱정돼다. These verbs both mean 'To worry' but the nuance is slightly differnt

components.target: 왜 아직도 안 자요? 내일 일찍 일어나야 하잖아요.
components.source: (Why are you still awake? You have to get up early tomorrow.)

components.target: 늦잠을 잘까 봐 걱정돼서 알람을 5개나 맞췄어요.
components.source: (I’m worried that I might oversleep, so I set five alarms.)

components.question: Is this a correct use of the grammar?

components.content: This grammatical structure can be used with either 걱정하다 or 걱정돼다. These verbs both mean 'To worry' but the nuance is slightly differnt

components.target: 왜 아직도 안 자요? 내일 일찍 일어나야 하잖아요.
components.source: (Why are you still awake? You have to get up early tomorrow.)

components.target: 늦잠을 잘까 봐 걱정돼서 알람을 5개나 맞췄어요.
components.source: (I’m worried that I might oversleep, so I set five alarms.)

components.question: Is this a correct use of the grammar?

components.content: In this lesson, we will learn how to add an intention to a sentence. You will be able to say 'I'm doing ___ in order to ____' or 'I intend to study later today'
The standard grammatical particle for this is:

components.source: (I planned/intended to go to the library today.)

components.target: 주말에 부모님과 같이 밥 먹으려고 약속했어요.
components.source: (I promised/arranged to have dinner with my parents on the weekend.)
